questions.question: Which pieces of the accepted comment-post HTTP contract should the dedicated auth/rate-limit test freeze: just the stable `comment` fields (`marketId`, `seq`, `accountId`, `body`) plus `201`, or also generated fields like `commentId`, `createdAt`, and the `meta.timestamp` envelope returned by `build_comment_post_acceptance_response(...)`?
questions.why_it_matters: Unlike the other protected POST routes, comment-post returns a `comment` object and `meta`, not a terminal `result` block. The step is specifically about first-class route-local coverage, so the exact assertion boundary determines whether the new test only checks a minimal happy-path shape or fully locks the route's accepted response contract.

questions.question: Should the dedicated comment-post test stop at the immediate HTTP response plus `assert_rate_limit_headers(...)`, or should it also verify persisted comment side effects such as `server.COMMENTS`, `MARKET_COMMENT_SEQUENCES`, or a follow-up `GET /v1/markets/{id}/comments` read?
questions.why_it_matters: There are currently no route-specific comment-post HTTP tests elsewhere in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`. This choice decides whether step 3 is only an auth/rate-limit response sentinel or also the first end-to-end proof that the accepted comment payload matches stored discussion-thread state.

questions.question: Since `test_protected_post_routes_emit_rate_limit_headers_on_first_success` already exercises comment-post in the shared breadth loop, is the new standalone test expected to reuse the same one-shot limiter setup and add route-local payload assertions only, or should it pin a different scenario such as trimmed-body normalization or a second posted comment with `seq == 2`?
questions.why_it_matters: The branch already has broad first-success header coverage for comment-post. The dedicated test needs to add unique value rather than duplicate the loop verbatim, and the chosen scenario determines what route-specific behavior becomes frozen.

questions.question: Is idempotency intentionally out of scope for this dedicated success test even though `handle_comment_post(...)` supports `idempotencyKey`, replay, conflict responses, and `meta.idempotencyKeyEcho`?
questions.why_it_matters: The current `comment_post_with_headers(...)` helper does not accept an idempotency key, and step 3 is framed as first-success auth/rate-limit coverage. Clarifying whether replay belongs here decides if the helper can stay simple and whether the test should assert only the fresh accepted path.

questions.question: Should this step remain success-path only for comment-post, leaving missing-agent, blank-agent, malformed-agent, and disabled-limiter no-header cases to the later absence-path work even though comment-post still lacks those route-specific negatives in `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests`?
questions.why_it_matters: The parent plan already separates success-path header work from absence-path preservation, but comment-post is still missing route-local auth failures. This answer keeps the patch scoped to one dedicated success test instead of starting the next phase inside the same class.

assumptions.assumption: This step should stay tests-only in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`; the shared HTTP adapter in `backend/server.py` already emits quota headers for successful protected writes, and comment-post is already included in the new breadth loop.
assumptions.if_wrong: A failing dedicated comment-post test would expose a real backend contract gap, and the work would need to expand beyond test coverage.

assumptions.assumption: The reset-safe happy-path fixture for the dedicated test is a fresh `POST /v1/markets/m1/comments` using the existing `comment_post_with_headers(...)` helper, an explicit agent id, and `RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN > 0`, because `setUp()` resets comments and `m1` starts active.
assumptions.if_wrong: The new test would need custom setup or a helper change before it could exercise the intended first-success contract reliably.

assumptions.assumption: The dedicated test should freeze that comment-post's accepted success shape is `{"comment": ..., "meta": ...}` without a terminal `result` block, because `build_comment_post_acceptance_response(...)` returns that distinct envelope.
assumptions.if_wrong: The implementation might overfit to current internals or miss a route-specific compatibility expectation elsewhere in the suite.

assumptions.assumption: The shared breadth test already serves as the exact first-success quota proof for comment-post, so the standalone test can focus on route-local accepted-comment behavior while still asserting the shared headers once.
assumptions.if_wrong: The dedicated test would need to duplicate more of the breadth loop's quota-only assertions to provide sufficient value.

assumptions.assumption: No child task is needed because the relevant plan context, test class, helper methods, and server-side comment contract are all already present in this checkout.
assumptions.if_wrong: A follow-up exploration task would be needed before implementation could be scoped confidently.
